explanation: Cast bridge distortion occurs when seating or occlusal forces push the metal beyond its elastic limit into plastic deformation, so the framework no longer returns to the intended fit. Pure occlusal discrepancy without plastic deformation does not permanently warp the casting.

Amalgam ditching deeper than about 0.5 mm indicates marginal breakdown and leakage risk; patching with composite over old amalgam is unreliable. Full replacement of the restoration is the appropriate management.

Divergent multi-unit implants need an open-tray (pick-up) transfer with two-piece copings so the impression captures true implant angulation without locking a closed-tray coping path. Closed-tray transfers fail when paths of draw diverge.

In lateral excursion, posterior group-function contacts (especially heavy balancing/working posterior loading) are more destructive than mutually protected canine guidance with disclusion of posteriors. Shallow inclines and canine guidance reduce lateral posterior stress.

For amalgam cores/build-ups, self-threading pins provide the greatest retention in dentin among classic pin types. Friction-lock and cemented pins retain less; self-shear describes a pin design feature, not the preferred retention class for endodontically treated molars needing pins.

Decreased freeway space means occlusal vertical dimension is too high (teeth meet too soon). Selective grinding of denture teeth (lab or chairside) can restore freeway space; relieving the intaglio alone does not correct OVD, and remake is not always first step.

Whole saliva contains electrolytes (including potassium), secretory IgA (antibodies), and antimicrobial proteins such as lysozyme. Free broad proteolytic enzyme activity is not a normal dominant salivary component in the way board keys frame this item, so proteolytic enzymes is the ‘not present’ choice.

A broken shallow amalgam in a small cavity is often best replaced with bonded composite for conservative tooth-colored repair with adhesive retention. Full amalgam replacement is unnecessary if remaining structure allows a bonded approach; GIC is weaker for stress-bearing areas.

In a high-caries pediatric patient sealing permanent molars, filled resin/flowable composite sealants offer better wear resistance than unfilled resin while still penetrating fissures when isolation is adequate. GIC is an alternative under moisture risk but the keyed choice is filled resin sealant.

Adolescent deep bite can be reduced with a removable appliance incorporating an anterior bite plane that allows posterior eruption and intrusion/retroclination of incisors. Fixed alone is not the only option named; the specific deep-bite tool among choices is the removable anterior bite plane.

Uncontrolled diabetes (HbA1c ~10) with acute apical abscess and urgent extraction raises infection risk; postoperative antibiotics are indicated after source control. Routine 1-hour amoxicillin IE-style prophylaxis alone is not the diabetes/abscess hinge.

Crown lengthening when keratinized gingiva is inadequate needs an apically repositioned flap (often with osseous recontouring) to expose tooth structure while preserving or relocating a band of attached gingiva. Simple gingivectomy removes keratinized tissue and is contraindicated.

A radial land on rotary endodontic instruments widens the peripheral contact, reduces aggressive engagement and taper lock, and thereby increases safety (less transportation/fracture risk). It does not primarily increase cutting efficiency.

MOD preparation that extends to the mesiobuccal cusp tip means that cusp is undermined and needs coverage. A ceramic onlay covers cusps; a ceramic inlay stays within cuspal inclines and is insufficient when the cusp tip is involved.

History of myocardial infarction more than about 60 days ago without ongoing acute issues places the patient in ASA III (severe systemic disease). ASA II is mild disease; ASA IV is constant threat to life (e.g., very recent MI, unstable angina).

Coronal microleakage is a major cause of endodontic failure; the integrity of the temporary (and later permanent) coronal seal strongly determines short- and long-term success, more than sealer brand or obturation technique alone.
